title: St. Lawrence Shrine
tagline: St. Lawrence Shrine — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: St. Lawrence Shrine is a much-loved spiritual town in Karnataka, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Lawrence Shrine Basilica or Attur Basilica (Portuguese: São Lourenço de Carcoal, English: St. Lawrence of Karkala) is a Roman Catholic church in Karkala, India. It was built in 1759 and is purported to be the site of many miracles.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Karkala
topPlaces.description: Karkala (Kannada: [kɑːɾkəɭə]) (Tulu: [kɑːɾlə]) is a town and the headquarters of eponymous Karkala taluk in the Udupi district of Karnataka state in India. It is well known as an important center of Jainism, with several ancient basadis (Jain temples) and the iconic 42-foot monolithic statue of Bahubali (Gommateshwara) dating back to the 15th century.

topPlaces.name: Chaturmukha Basadi, Karkala
topPlaces.description: Chaturmukha Basadi is a symmetrical Jain temple situated in Karkala, Karnataka, India. It is one of the most famous monuments in Karkala.

topPlaces.name: Sri Venkataramana Temple, Karkala
topPlaces.description: Sri Venkataramana Temple, Karkala, also known as Padutirupathi, is a temple in Karnataka, India. It was built by Goud Saraswat Brahmins of Karkala and was installed on 25 April 1537.

topPlaces.name: Yennehole
topPlaces.description: Yennehole is a small village near Ajekar in Karkala taluk of Udupi district in India. There is a small river, Swarna, running through this village by same name.

topPlaces.name: Gommateshwara statue, Karkala
topPlaces.description: Gommateshwara statue, Karkala (ಗೊಮ್ಮಟೇಶ್ವರ ಪ್ರತಿಮೆ ಕಾರ್ಕಳ) is located at Karkala in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is the second tallest statue of Bahubali in the world with the largest statue located at Shravanabelagola.

topPlaces.name: Sanoor
topPlaces.description: Sanoor is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. During the British Raj, the village was headed by the Patels (Patler).

topPlaces.name: Nitte
topPlaces.description: Nitte is a village in Karkala Taluk, Udupi District, Karnataka, India. It is located on the way from Padubidri to Karkala, about 26 kilometres southeast of the district capital Udupi, and 7 kilometres southwest of the taluk capital Karkala.

topPlaces.name: Attur, Udupi
topPlaces.description: Attur is a small village in Karkal taluk, Udupi District, Karnataka state, India. It comes under Nitte panchayat and is a part of Mysore Division.

items.note: Karkala (Kannada: [kɑːɾkəɭə]) (Tulu: [kɑːɾlə]) is a town and the headquarters of eponymous Karkala taluk in the Udupi district of Karnataka state in India. It is well known as an important center of Jainism, with several ancient basadis (Jain temples) and the iconic 42-foot monolithic statue of Bahubali (Gommateshwara) dating back to the 15th century.
Chaturmukha Basadi is a symmetrical Jain temple situated in Karkala, Karnataka, India. It is one of the most famous monuments in Karkala.
Sri Venkataramana Temple, Karkala, also known as Padutirupathi, is a temple in Karnataka, India. It was built by Goud Saraswat Brahmins of Karkala and was installed on 25 April 1537.
Yennehole is a small village near Ajekar in Karkala taluk of Udupi district in India. There is a small river, Swarna, running through this village by same name.

Gommateshwara statue, Karkala (ಗೊಮ್ಮಟೇಶ್ವರ ಪ್ರತಿಮೆ ಕಾರ್ಕಳ) is located at Karkala in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is the second tallest statue of Bahubali in the world with the largest statue located at Shravanabelagola.
Sanoor is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. During the British Raj, the village was headed by the Patels (Patler).

Nitte is a village in Karkala Taluk, Udupi District, Karnataka, India. It is located on the way from Padubidri to Karkala, about 26 kilometres southeast of the district capital Udupi, and 7 kilometres southwest of the taluk capital Karkala.
Attur is a small village in Karkal taluk, Udupi District, Karnataka state, India. It comes under Nitte panchayat and is a part of Mysore Division.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Lawrence Shrine?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Lawrence Shrine is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Lawrence Shrine?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (29 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (40 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Lawrence Shrine?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Lawrence Shrine at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Lawrence Shrine?
faq.a: Stays in St. Lawrence Shrine start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Lawrence Shrine?
faq.a: The top attractions are Karkala, Chaturmukha Basadi, Karkala, Sri Venkataramana Temple, Karkala, Yennehole, Gommateshwara statue, Karkala.
